attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches early from distance (200m+) when positioning is compromised, relying on sustained power rather than explosive acceleration. Wins often come from chaotic finales where traditional trains disintegrate.
team_tactics: Alpecin team does heavy chase work and attempts organized leadouts, but execution frequently breaks down in final 200m. Shows tactical flexibility to win without traditional train when needed, often capitalizing on positioning chaos rather than structured sprint setups.
terrain_preference: Pure flat stages with technical finales suit best. Exposed on moderate gradients (dropped 20km out multiple times) and struggles when courses feature roundabouts/corners inside 2km that unwind sprint trains. Weight/climbing vulnerability evident on Ardennes-style circuits.
vulnerability: Poor positioning in final 500m repeatedly costs results—specifically at corner exits and in chaotic technical finales. When leadout train fails in final 200m, lacks pure acceleration to recover. Dropped on even moderate gradients with 8-20km remaining.
key_patterns: Positioning errors in final 500m recurring weakness, especially at corner exits (4+ instances)
Wins from chaos when structured trains fail, demonstrates superior tactical flexibility (3 wins this way)
Dropped on moderate climbs 8-20km from finish despite being sprinter (3 instances)
Team does extensive chase work but leadout execution breaks down final 200m repeatedly
Long-range sprint strategy from distance when positioning compromised, relies on sustained power
Course reconnaissance pays dividends—barrier-side positioning advantage yielded UAE Tour wins
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 wins show raw speed intact, but Paris-Nice positioning failure (caught 500m out) suggests persistent technical weakness remains unresolved despite tactical flexibility improvements
